title: Interview Templates
description: Essential C interview algorithm templates — binary search, two-pointer, sliding window, and prefix sum patterns you'll use in nearly every coding interview.

sections.title: Binary search
sections.explanation: Binary search finds a target in a sorted array in O(log n). Use two pointers `lo` and `hi`. Compute `mid = lo + (hi - lo) / 2` (avoids overflow). Compare `arr[mid]` with target: go left or right accordingly. Return the index or -1 if not found.

exercise.prompt: Implement binary search on sorted array {1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13}. Search for 7 (print its index) and 6 (print -1 for not found).

validation.message: 7 is at index 3, 6 is not found (-1)
Use standard binary search template with overflow-safe mid calculation

exercise.hint: mid = lo + (hi - lo) / 2 avoids integer overflow. 7 is at index 3. 6 isn't in the array.

sections.title: Two-pointer technique
sections.explanation: Two pointers move through a sorted array from both ends (or two arrays) to find pairs or partition elements in O(n). Classic uses: pair with target sum, removing duplicates, container with most water. Move left pointer right when sum is too small, right pointer left when too large.

exercise.prompt: Find two indices in sorted array {1, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10} that sum to 12 using two pointers. Print the two values.

validation.message: Use two pointers moving inward: increment lo if sum too small, decrement hi if too large

sections.title: Sliding window
sections.explanation: Sliding window maintains a window [left, right] over an array, expanding right and shrinking left to find optimal subarrays. **Fixed-size window**: move both pointers together. **Variable-size**: expand right, shrink left while a condition is violated. Track window state incrementally.

exercise.prompt: Find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size 3 in {2, 1, 5, 1, 3, 2}. Print the maximum sum.

validation.message: Use sliding window: add new element, subtract old element, track maximum

exercise.hint: Slide window of size 3: add arr[i], subtract arr[i-3]. Sums: 8, 7, 9, 6. Maximum is 9.

sections.title: Prefix sum
sections.explanation: Prefix sum precomputes cumulative sums so any range sum `arr[l..r]` is answered in O(1): `prefix[r+1] - prefix[l]`. Build with `prefix[i+1] = prefix[i] + arr[i]`. This is essential for subarray sum problems and 2D DP.

exercise.prompt: Build a prefix sum array for {2, 4, 6, 8, 10}. Use it to compute and print the sum of elements from index 1 to 3 (inclusive), then the sum from index 0 to 4.

validation.message: Build prefix array incrementally, query with prefix[r+1] - prefix[l]
